channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Warsh promises inflation will be a 'thing of the past,' cites benefits of AI investment boom
item.description: Warsh pledged Tuesday to "get monetary policy right" and defeat the inflation that has bedeviled the central bank for the past five years.

item.title: Bank earnings live updates: Jamie Dimon says AI has helped JPMorgan cut up to 40% of jobs in certain roles
item.description: The five banks releasing earnings Tuesday morning have reported strong revenue from equities trading.

item.title: Consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, less than expected as energy prices eased
item.description: The consumer price index in June was expected to increase 3.8% from a year ago.

item.title: S&P 500 trades higher after light inflation data, but gain held in check by IBM drop, oil jump: Live updates
item.description: The S&P 500 rose on Tuesday after June inflation data came in weaker than expected.

item.title: Earnings season plays: Profit expectations are growing for these stocks while their valuations get cheaper
item.description: Investors can find opportunities in stocks where declining share prices are pushing valuations below their historical average.

item.title: IBM stock craters 23% after issuing second-quarter earnings warning
item.description: CEO blamed the shortfall on weakness in the software and infrastructure business because clients shifted money toward hardware purchases.

item.title: Tuesday, July 14, 2026: Jim Cramer discusses his next moves for the Investing Club
Sen. Tim Scott wants to hear from Warsh on data centers and artificial intelligence
item.description: Sen. Tim Scott, R-S.C., will host Kevin Warsh before the Senate Banking Committee on Wednesday for his first time testifying as Fed chairman.

item.title: Google DeepMind chief Demis Hassabis calls for U.S. to spearhead AI standards body
item.description: Tech giant's AI boss said "urgent action" was needed as AI capabilities advanced.

item.title: Inflation had its biggest monthly decline in six years. What Wall Street is saying now
item.description: June's inflation report was not what many on the Street expected, in a good way.

item.title: United Airlines' new upsell: Keeping other travelers out of the middle seat
item.description: United Airlines will allow customers to pay more to keep the middle seat open on its Airbus A321XLRs

item.title: Frontier Airlines to debut in-flight Wi-Fi in 2027 with SpaceX's Starlink
item.description: Frontier is signing with Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi, with service debuting as early as next year.

item.title: SpaceX alumni building remote-controlled construction equipment land $115 million fund round
item.description: TerraFirma on Tuesday said it raised $115 to support new hires and the building of a new factory and mission control center.

item.title: Warren Buffett excludes Gates Foundation from his annual donations of Berkshire stock
item.description: Warren Buffett omitted the Gates Foundation from his annual charitable stock gifts.

item.title: Global shipping industry sounds the alarm over Trump’s Hormuz toll plan
item.description: For shipping giant Hapag-Lloyd, it is "fundamentally wrong" to charge tolls for passage through international waters, regardless of the country in charge.

item.title: Here are Tuesday's biggest analyst calls: Nvidia, SK Hynix, Apple, SpaceX, Broadcom, AMD, Netflix & more
item.description: Here are the biggest calls on Wall Street on Tuesday.

item.title: Consumers turn to buy now, pay later for essential expenses — with growing risks
item.description: Some consumers are using buy now, pay later for essential expenses like groceries, rent and utility bills. But more BNPL users have recently had a late payment.

item.title: Mike Santoli: Why eye-popping earnings expectations have failed to lift the market for two months
item.description: The mantra heading into second-quarter results is that another display of stupendous growth of 24% over a year earlier makes equities appear quite reasonably priced.

item.title: Business declares war on Pentagon stock buyback, dividend restrictions moving in Senate
item.description: The provision was pushed by Sen. Elizabeth Warren, D-Mass., and was included in the National Defense Authorization Act on a bipartisan basis.

item.title: Jim Cramer's top 10 things to watch in the stock market Tuesday
item.description: S&P 500 futures are up after June inflation data came in weaker than expected and earnings season kicked off with the banks.

item.title: World Cup's biggest spenders show up late as semifinals drive host city travel boom
item.description: World Cup travel demand to U.S. host cities is spiking as the tournament reaches the semifinal stage.

item.title: Need a business loan? SoFi now offers up to $250,000 — here's how it compares
item.description: SoFi charges no application, origination or prepayment fees on its new small business loans, but rivals offer larger loans.

item.title: Taiwan's second-largest chipmaker starts mass production in Singapore; Citi sees improving outlook
item.description: Taiwan’s UMC has kicked off mass production of silicon photonics wafers at its Singapore facility amid positive Citi forecast.

item.title: TikTok policy chief defends safety measures amid EU push to limit children's social media access
item.description: TikTok's policy chief defended the platform's safety measures as the European Union pushes ahead with restricting children's access to social media.

item.title: This trucking stock has doubled in 2026. BMO says it has more room to run
item.description: RXO's shares are likely to advance as the company notches strong earnings amid a tightening trucking market, per BMO Capital Markets.

item.title: A French underwear brand is taking on fast fashion — with an IPO
item.description: Le Slip Français will make its stock market debut in Paris on Tuesday, betting that consumers will pay for locally made clothing amid competition from Shein.

item.title: Retail-trader 'bottleneck bros' eyeing AI supply chain can't wait for SK Hynix options
item.description: For many of those retail tech traders, SK Hynix's memory business reminds them of Micron – the paragon of the supply-chain bottleneck thesis.

item.title: Jim Cramer says these 'Pavlovian trades' are the stocks that benefit when oil spikes
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said investors have gravitated toward a familiar group of stocks as oil prices climb.

item.title: Trump to claim declassified intel reveals 2020 election interference: MS NOW
item.description: Trump said he will make a "Speech to the Nation on Thursday evening, at 9 P.M. Eastern," without providing further details.

item.title: 'Nobody underwrote for that': Private credit faces a key test as higher rates squeeze borrowers
item.description: Private credit is facing a new pressure point from elevated rates, which could hit under-pressure borrowers.
